content: Specimen detail reference image for Oplophorus novaezeelandiae (Keeled flattened red prawn) from the species identification page. PDF page 170, printed page 167. Associated diagnostic features: Laterally-flattened prawn with distinct spine on third abdominal segment. Rostrum approximately same length as carapace, with six dorsal spines close to the base and three to four ventral spines. No visible telson spines, but three tiny projections at tip of telson. No spinose appendage. Outer margin of scaphocerite (flattened appendage near mouth) smooth, no barb on inner margin. Two short lateral keels along sides of carapace below eyes. Associated colour description: Deep red. Associated size description: 60–100 mm total length. Associated distribution: West Coast (> 400 m) of South Africa. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Whole-specimen reference image for Heterocarpus laevigatus (Smooth nylon shrimp) from the species identification page. PDF page 171, printed page 168. Associated diagnostic features: Distinctive appearance with swollen cephalothorax, marked dorsal keel cut into about five teeth, plus two marked lateral keels, the lower produced into a sharp spine anteriorly. Carapace pitted. Rostrum elongate and curved strongly upwards, one tooth at the base above eye, rest of dorsal margin smooth, ventral margin with 10 teeth. Abdominal segments not keeled. Associated colour description: Orange-red. Associated size description: 110-130 mm total length. Associated distribution: South and West Coasts of South Africa, widespread in Indo-Pacific and off West Africa and Brazil. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Specimen detail reference image for Glyphus marsupialis (Kangaroo shrimp) from the species identification page. PDF page 173, printed page 170. Associated diagnostic features: Large red shrimp. Carapace with dorsal ridge terminating in a short triangular rostrum. Two pairs of scissor-like chelae with fingers bearing numerous sharp, toothlike scales. Abdomen of female swollen (hence common name). Known to bioluminesce. Carnivorous. Associated colour description: Dark red. Associated size description: Up to 160 mm total length. Associated distribution: West and South Coasts of South Africa. Widely distributed in Pacific, Indian and (less so) Atlantic Oceans, benthic on sandy seabeds at 500-1100 m. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

Abdomen of female swollen (hence common name). Known to bioluminesce. Carnivorous. Associated colour description: Dark red. Associated size description: Up to 160 mm total length. Associated distribution: West and South Coasts of South Africa. Widely distributed in Pacific, Indian and (less so) Atlantic Oceans, benthic on sandy seabeds at 500-1100 m. Extracted image dimensions: 156x141.

content: Specimen detail reference image for Pasiphaea sp. 1 (Glass shrimp) from the species identification page. PDF page 174, printed page 171. Associated diagnostic features: Small, translucent shrimp with orange trim along dorsal and ventral carapace varying in coverage, telson, antennae and tips of chelipeds. First and second pair of pereopods chelate. Associated colour description: Translucent to white, with orange colouration on edges of claws, tail and carapace, which can cover much of the body. Associated size description: Up to 90 mm body length, but usually smaller (30 mm). Associated distribution: West and South Coasts of South Africa. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Specimen detail reference image for Pasiphaea sp. 2 (Ventrally flattened prawn) from the species identification page. PDF page 175, printed page 172. Associated diagnostic features: Ventrally flattened, very short triangle rostrum with tiny dorsal spine. Large, well-developed fine chelae on first and second pereopods. Abdominal segment two overlaps with segment one. Considerably larger and more ventrally flattened than Pasiphaea sp. 1. Associated colour description: Often red thorax with white tail. Can have orange- red colouration around edges, or be completely white, or completely red to orange. Associated size description: Average 160 mm total length including rostrum. Associated distribution: West and South Coasts of South Africa. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Specimen detail reference image for Calocaris barnardi (Snapper shrimp) from the species identification page. PDF page 176, printed page 173. Associated diagnostic features: Resembles a sand-prawn in overall appearance. Carapace with short, horizontal, pointed rostrum lacking marginal teeth distally, but with lateral edges upturned, and with untoothed medial keel. Rostrum at base with four teeth on either side. First two pairs of pereopods chelate, the first pair much larger and more robust. Abdomen elongate and lacking ornamentation; exopod of uropod with keel. Telson longer than broad, strongly setose (with bristles) along margin, apex broadly rounded. Associated colour description: Bright to pale orange. Associated size description: Average 80 mm total length, including claw. Associated distribution: Namibia to West Coast of South Africa. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Specimen detail reference image for Stereomastis sculpta (Deep-sea blind lobster/Sea cockroach) from the species identification page. PDF page 177, printed page 174. Associated diagnostic features: Unusual, heavily sculptured, blind, widespread deep- sea crustacean. Carapace with median keel, lateral keels and transverse median ridge, all produced into sharp spines. Abdominal segments 1 to 5 with keels forming large forwardly-directed spines, increasing in size from first to fourth segment. Slender, elongate claws held forwards. Associated colour description: Mostly pink with darker patches on telson, but can also be nearly all white with red patches on telson and parapodia tips. Associated size description: Up to 130 mm body length. Associated distribution: Predominantly West Coast of South Africa. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Specimen detail reference image for Munida benguela (Striped squat lobster) from the species identification page. PDF page 178, printed page 175. Associated diagnostic features: Small lobster-type crustacean. Tail often folded underneath abdomen. Carapace and abdominal segments with transverse ridges. Distinctly striped pattern on thorax. Chelipeds (claws) as long as thorax and abdomen combined. Associated colour description: Orange-and-white striped pattern, tail white. Associated size description: 50-60 mm in total length. Associated distribution: Namibia to KwaZulu-Natal, South Africa. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Distribution map for Dardanus arrosor (Striated hermit crab) from the species identification page. PDF page 179, printed page 176. Associated diagnostic features: Unmistakable, with transverse, scaly striations on chelae and pereopods. Left cheliped larger than right one. Associated colour description: Orange to brown, eyestalks orange with two red bands. Associated size description: Can grow to a large shield length of 75 mm, total length 250 mm. One of the largest South African hermit crabs. Associated distribution: All along South African coasts, common on South Coast shelf region, from 30-290 m. Extracted image dimensions: 156x141.

content: Distribution map for Paguristes sp. (Agulhas bank hermit) from the species identification page. PDF page 180, printed page 177. Associated diagnostic features: Left chela slightly larger. Chelae and pereopods with corneous tips and irregularly covered with prominent tubercles, which end in a brown, corneous spine. Not heavily covered with hairs. Associated colour description: Orange, with green eyes. Tubercles on pereopods and chelae pinkish-white. Associated size description: Up to 9 mm shield length, total length 100 mm. content: Specimen detail reference image for Anapagurus hendersoni (Blue-lined hermit) from the species identification page. PDF page 181, printed page 178. Associated diagnostic features: Enlarged right cheliped; dorsal surface of chela with small tubercles and high, spinose ridge proximally; carpus long and with row of small spines mesially. Left cheliped very slender, palm of chela (weak) and carpus with longitudinal double row of spines dorsally. Best identifiable by colouration. Associated colour description: Two colour morphs exist in South African waters having one of two background colourations – either cream or brownish with the same colour patterns. Shield orange to brown in both forms, corneas of eyes dark greenish to yellow-turquoise. Characteristic features are the translucent blue longitudinal stripes on the ventral margin of the propodi of the pereopods, and a maroon dot on the mesioventral (inner side) of each chela; the right a large dot and left a smaller dot (not visible in frontal view, sometimes less pronounced in the cream colour morph). Associated size description: Up to 40 mm total length. Associated distribution: South African endemic. West Coast of South Africa to KwaZulu-Natal, 9-226 m. Extracted image dimensions: 237x191.

content: Distribution map for Pagurus cuanensis (hairy hermit) from the species identification page. PDF page 182, printed page 179. Associated diagnostic features: Right chela distinctively larger than left. Chelae and pereopods heavily covered with setae (hairs), usually concealing the armature. Palm of right hand with three to four rows of medium to strong spines, of which the median row is usually the strongest. Associated colour description: Pereopods and chelae brown, completely covered with earth-coloured setae. Merus of chelipeds (see line diagram p. 136) reddish, sprinkled with whitish spots. Eyestalks yellow to orange. Second antennae reddish-brown with white rings. Offshore specimens duller in colour than inshore (False Bay) individuals; often in old and overgrown shells. Associated size description: Up to 8 mm shield length, total length 50 mm. Associated distribution: Reported from Vema Seamount (Namibian West Coast), False Bay, Cape St. Blaize, Mossel Bay, Durban and KwaZulu-Natal to 130 m. Common on Agulhas Bank. Extracted image dimensions: 156x141.

content: Specimen detail reference image for Pagurus liochele (Blue-faced hermit) from the species identification page. PDF page 183, printed page 180. Associated diagnostic features: Right chela distinctively larger than left. Palm of right hand with row of blunt spines on dorsomesial margin and surface covered with low blue tubercles. Stronger row of white-blue tubercles adjacent to cutting edge of fixed finger. Colour diagnostic. Specimens from South Coast trawls mostly in shells occupying cavities in an undescribed species of Suberites sponge (see picture). Associated colour description: Eyestalks orange at base with distal half characteristically cobalt-blue. Dark purple chelipeds covered with cobalt-blue tubercles. Propodi of pereopods with cobalt-blue ring distally, dactyls with reddish longitudinal stripes. Second antennae red with white rings. Associated size description: Up to 7 mm shield length, total length 40 mm. Associated distribution: Southern African endemic. Orange River to Transkei, South Africa, littoral to 110 m. Sometimes caught in inshore trawls. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Distribution map for Propagurus deprofundis (Orange keeled hermit) from the species identification page. PDF page 184, printed page 181. Associated diagnostic features: Chelae uniformly orange and spiny, right larger than left. Palm covered with six irregular rows of spines, accompanied by long and stiff setae. Carpus with irregular row of strong spines on dorso-mesial margin. Mesial face of propodus of second pereopod with longitudinal keel (see pictures). Associated colour description: Pereopods, eyestalks and shield bright orange, corneas of eyes black. Tips of dactyls and fingers corneous and black. Associated size description: Up to 9.3 mm shield length, total length 80 mm. Associated distribution: South Coast of South Africa. Single individuals occasionally caught in deep trawls along Agulhas Shelf. Elsewhere recorded from 200-915 m and found in variety of gastropod shells. Extracted image dimensions: 156x141.

PDF page 184, printed page 181. Associated diagnostic features: Chelae uniformly orange and spiny, right larger than left. Palm covered with six irregular rows of spines, accompanied by long and stiff setae. Carpus with irregular row of strong spines on dorso-mesial margin. Mesial face of propodus of second pereopod with longitudinal keel (see pictures). Associated colour description: Pereopods, eyestalks and shield bright orange, corneas of eyes black. Tips of dactyls and fingers corneous and black. Associated size description: Up to 9.3 mm shield length, total length 80 mm. Associated distribution: South Coast of South Africa. Single individuals occasionally caught in deep trawls along Agulhas Shelf. Elsewhere recorded from 200-915 m and found in variety of gastropod shells. Extracted image dimensions: 141x192.

content: Whole-specimen reference image for Goreopagurus poorei (Broad-clawed hermit) from the species identification page. PDF page 185, printed page 182. Associated diagnostic features: Immediately identifiable by very uniquely shaped, large right cheliped (even larger in males, as depicted), with carpus dorsoventrally flattened and produced to the sides, flared, with sharp spines around the inner margin. Chela long and elongated, not bearing any spines. Left cheliped slender. Eyestalks short and stout, about half the length of shield. Associated colour description: General background colouration orange. Shield light orange, fading to white medially and near rostrum. Eyestalks mottled orange and white, distally white near black corneas. Chelipeds mostly orange with pale orange chela and fingers. Pereopods with weakly-defined orange stripe on lateral and mesial faces. Associated size description: Up to 60 mm in total length. Associated distribution: Along edge of Agulhas shelf, South Africa, 334-622 m. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Specimen detail reference image for Paragiopagurus atkinsonae (Green-eyed hermit) from the species identification page. PDF page 186, printed page 183. Associated diagnostic features: Very similar to S. dimorphus, but smaller, with same dimorphism: large right cheliped in males, in females shorter and chela rounded to oval shape. Without any obvious distinguishing characters, but overall appearance different to S. dimorphus. Pereopods longer and more slender, eyes shorter. Right cheliped not very setose. Inhabits same colonial anemone as S. dimorphus. Not recorded from gastropod shells. Distinctive colouration. Associated colour description: More uniformly orange than S. dimorphus. Chelipeds orange, with white tubercules or spines. Segments of pereopods commonly with dorsal white spots (see arrows). Eyes usually green and eyestalks with orange pattern dorsally, not forming clear, continuous orange-red stripe, as in S. dimorphus. Associated size description: Shield length < 10 mm; total length up to 50 mm. Associated distribution: South African endemic. Localised area on the West Coast of South Africa, not reported from South Coast. Known from depths 199-277 m. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Specimen detail reference image for Parapagurus andreui (Sun-anemone hermit) from the species identification page. PDF page 187, printed page 184. Associated diagnostic features: Right cheliped very large (missing in photographed specimen), and both chelipeds densely setose. Shield about as broad as long and usually well calcified. Eyestalks less than half the length of shield. Very few morphological features for identification on deck, but colour might be characteristic. Known to occupy zooanthids that have > 10 polyps arranged in a circle around the lower margin of the shell. However, a few other species might occupy the same type of zooanthid. Associated colour description: In South Africa only known from photographed specimen. Shield and bases of cephalic appendage (antennae and eyestalks) white-washed orange to mouldy white. Chelipeds appear yellowish due to heavy setation, walking legs brownish orange, colour intensified in dactyls. Associated size description: Between 100-120 mm total length. Associated distribution: West Coast of South Africa; 731 m. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Specimen detail reference image for Parapagurus bouvieri (hairy-clawed hermit) from the species identification page. PDF page 188, printed page 185. Associated diagnostic features: Both left and right cheliped densely setose, right cheliped much longer. Weakly calcified lateral faces of meri of second and third pereopods diagnostic. Exclusively inhabit pseudoshells of a single species of colonial zooanthid, which form a smooth, slimy, pinkish cloak. Zoanthid polyps arranged around bottom margin of pseudoshell (unlike evenly- distributed polyps of the epizoanthid colonising Sympagurus dimorphus and Paragiopagurus atkinsonae). Associated colour description: Adult with conspicuous white band along dorsal and ventral margins of pereopods. Ventral faces of pereopods orange. Chelae often with orange- pink fingertips. Some specimens have pale orange or yellow pereopods without distinctive colour patterns. Associated size description: Up to 15 mm shield length, overall size up to 100 mm. Associated distribution: Southern African endemic. Namibia to Cape St Francis, South Africa, 63-814 m (preferred depth range 400-499 m). Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Specimen detail reference image for Sympagurus dimorphus (Dimorphic hermit) from the species identification page. PDF page 189, printed page 186. Associated diagnostic features: Right cheliped much larger than left. Right one sexually dimorphic (two forms), massively enlarged in male where it cannot be retracted into shell. Carpus with dorsal row of spines. Most, but not all, individuals inhabit ‘pseudoshells’ made of epizoanthids (colonial anemones). Pseudoshell coarse in texture (gritty) and light brown; > 10 orange nodules (polyps) of different sizes unevenly distributed over entire surface. Associated colour description: Colour variable orange-red. Chelipeds from pale orange to almost bright red, but with cream spines or tubercles. Propodus and carpus of pereopods usually with longitudinal white stripes; sometimes entirely white. Meri of chelipeds and pereopods white with orange-red patches. Eyestalks dorsally with orange-red longitudinal stripe. Associated size description: Up to 30 mm shield length; overall size up to 100 mm. Associated distribution: Southern African endemic. Namibia to Plettenberg Bay, South Africa, 30-814 m (preferred depth range 200-249 m). Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

Right one sexually dimorphic (two forms), massively enlarged in male where it cannot be retracted into shell. Carpus with dorsal row of spines. content: Specimen detail reference image for Lithodes ferox (Fierce king crab) from the species identification page. PDF page 190, printed page 187. Associated diagnostic features: Three major pairs of pereopods visible (remaining two greatly reduced and hidden), plus chelipeds. Carapace rounded, becoming more triangular and pointed anteriorly, both gastric and cardiac regions of carapace with four prominent square-patterned spines. Rostrum strongly produced and bifid (rarely simple), with a pair of dorsal spines on corneal level. Right cheliped slightly larger, and larger in males. Chelipeds and pereopods with variously sized, strong spines. Associated colour description: Bright red to pale pink or orange in colour, with reddened dactyls of pereopods. Associated size description: Up to 65-70 mm carapace width; pereopods up to 170 mm long. Associated distribution: West and South Coasts of South Africa. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Specimen detail reference image for Neolithodes asperrimus (rough stone crab) from the species identification page. PDF page 191, printed page 188. Associated diagnostic features: Three pairs of pereopods visible (remaining two greatly reduced and hidden), plus chelipeds. Carapace with large and small spines, upper surface thickly sprinkled with small, sharp granules; gastric region of carapace with four central prominent spines in shape of a square and one additional lateral spine on each side, one single smaller spine in centre of square; cardiac area with four prominent square-patterned spines. Rostrum with one simple upward slanting spine and two dorsal spines near base. Chelipeds and pereopods thickly covered with sharp granules, more so in females than in males. Associated colour description: Orange. Associated size description: Large; carapace width up to 200 mm; pereopods up to 500 mm long. Associated distribution: West Coast of South Africa and northwards to Mauritania. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Specimen detail reference image for Neolithodes capensis (cape stone crab) from the species identification page. PDF page 192, printed page 189. Associated diagnostic features: Three pairs of pereopods visible (remaining two greatly reduced and hidden), plus chelipeds. Carapace with scattered small spines amongst larger ones. Gastric region of carapace with six prominent, hexagonally-arranged spines, cardiac region with two pairs of spines, followed by a single median one. Chelipeds and pereopods with surface somewhat smooth, scattered small and larger spines. Associated colour description: Deep brick red. Associated size description: Large; carapace up to 200 mm wide; pereopods up to 500 mm long. Associated distribution: Endemic. West Coast of South Africa. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Specimen detail reference image for Vitjazmaia latidactyla (horned eyestalk deep-water crab) from the species identification page. PDF page 193, printed page 190. Associated diagnostic features: Carapace nearly round, covered with small spines and numerous regular spinules, all curved anteriorly. Rostrum long, with long rostral spine and a pair of pseudo-rostral spines. Pseudo-rostral spines with two small spines at base of each. One very strong, long spine outside each eye, and one above each eye. Cluster of spiniform horns (2-5) visible on the eye stalk. Five pairs (chelipeds included) of very long and flat pereopods. Walking legs with surfaces covered with small, sharp spinules. Second pair of pereopods with long, sharp spines. Males with stronger and heavier tapered pincers. Associated colour description: Pale orange to peach, with darker spicules on carapace. Dactyls darker in colour. Associated size description: Up to 150 mm carapace width. Associated distribution: West and South Coasts of South Africa, common in New Zealand and some West Indian Ocean regions. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Specimen detail reference image for Platymaia turbynei (Three-spined spider crab) from the species identification page. PDF page 194, printed page 191. Associated diagnostic features: Carapace rounded in shape, length slightly greater than width, surface with several small acute spines. Rostrum trispinose, with one major spine projecting forward, markedly overarching the anterior margin of carapace. Inner margin of orbit without spine. Buccal cavity with blunt denticulate tooth at outer angle. Chelipeds elongate in males. Pereopods very long and slender, second and third pereopods with long and medium-long spines, respectively, dactyls and propodi of fourth and fifth pereopods with fringes of long, fine setae along ventral margins. Associated colour description: Pink, pale orange to salmon pink; legs with very broad orange bands. Associated size description: Carapace width up to 45 mm. Associated distribution: West and South Coasts of South Africa, Eastern Cape and KwaZulu-Natal, 200-960 m. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

Pereopods very long and slender, second and third pereopods with long and medium-long spines, respectively, dactyls and propodi of fourth and fifth pereopods with fringes of long, fine setae along ventral margins. Associated colour description: Pink, pale orange to salmon pink; legs with very broad orange bands. Associated size description: Carapace width up to 45 mm. Associated distribution: West and South Coasts of South Africa, Eastern Cape and KwaZulu-Natal, 200-960 m. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Specimen detail reference image for Achaeopsis spinulosa (Short-spined/hotlips spider crab) from the species identification page. PDF page 195, printed page 192. Associated diagnostic features: Carapace pear-shaped, broad posteriorly, tapering strongly anteriorly. Two short, stubby rostral spines extend to end of peduncle (base) apex of antennae 2. Spines slightly divergent and widely separated proximally. Strong, erect median spine on gastric region, stronger spine on cardiac region, smaller tubercle or spine on antero-lateral portion of gastric region. Total of eight spines clearly visible on dorsal carapace, similar to D. thomsoni. No visible spines at apex of fourth joint of second to fifth pereopods. Pereopods very long and slender, chelipeds rounded, bulbous. Associated colour description: Orange to salmon colour when alive, with white and red mottled chelipeds. Pereopods with wide bands of orange and white (often faint). Associated size description: Up to 20 mm carapace width. Associated distribution: West and South Coasts of South Africa. Widely distributed in Atlantic and Indian Oceans, usually occurring shallower than D. thomsoni. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Specimen detail reference image for Dorhynchus thomsoni (Long-spined spider crab) from the species identification page. PDF page 196, printed page 193. Associated diagnostic features: Carapace pear-shaped with strong, erect median spine on gastric region and stronger spine on cardiac region. Smaller spines on antero-lateral portion of gastric region. Total of eight spines on carapace, distinct, but not as pronounced as those of Macropodia falcifera, which has only four dorsal spines. Two rostral spines, slightly divergent distally, but close together, longer than Achaeopsis spinulosa. Rostral spines extend distinctly beyond peduncle (base) apex of antennae 2. Chelipeds more slender than A. spinulosa and rostral spines longer and closer together. Pereopods very long, with dorsodistal spine on merus of second to fifth pair (see red circles, distinguishes between D. thomsoni and A. spinulosa). Associated colour description: Orange to salmon when live, with white and red mottling on chelipeds. Pereopods with wide bands of orange and white (often faint). Associated size description: Up to 20 mm carapace width. Associated distribution: Predominantly West Coast, but do occur on South Coast of South Africa. Widely distributed in Atlantic and Indian Oceans, usually deeper than A. spinulosa. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Distribution map for Macropodia falcifera (cape long-rostrum spider crab) from the species identification page. PDF page 197, printed page 194. Associated diagnostic features: Carapace with single long erect spine in gastric region and on cardiac region, with two smaller spines on each dorso-lateral edge. Long, elongated rostrum of two sharp appressed (close together) spines, extending well beyond end of antennal peduncle (base), usually to end of flagellum of antennae 2. Eyes on long stalks, situated at distal end of extended carapace. Apex of merus of pereopods has either three, two or one small spine(s) – not to be confused with D. thomsoni, which has a single spine. Associated colour description: Pale orange to pink or red, often with darker red speckled chelipeds. Associated size description: Carapace up to 15 mm width. Associated distribution: Widespread, West and South Coasts of South Africa, although predominantly South Coast. Extracted image dimensions: 156x141.

content: Specimen detail reference image for Macropodia formosa (cape long-legged spider crab) from the species identification page. PDF page 198, printed page 195. Associated diagnostic features: Carapace pear-shaped, with single long erect spine in gastric region and another on cardiac region, plus two smaller spines on each dorso-lateral edge. Rostrum short, not extending beyond end of peduncle (base). Spines present on basal joints of antenna. Apex of merus of pereopods has either three, two or one small spine(s) present – not to be confused with D. thomsoni, which has a single spine. Associated colour description: Pale orange to pink or red, often with darker red speckled chelipeds. Associated size description: Carapace up to 20 mm wide. Associated distribution: South African endemic. Widespread, predominantly South Coast of South Africa to Mozambique. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Specimen detail reference image for Latreillia metanesa (candycane crab) from the species identification page. PDF page 199, printed page 196. Associated diagnostic features: Very distinctive, with small, pear-shaped carapace bearing dorsal knobs. Rostrum triangular and consisting of three long spikes, one projecting medially forward and two laterally. Eyes disproportionately large and borne on extremely elongate eyestalks that are composed of two parts, a proximal slender section and a second much stouter section. Long spindly pereopods appear out of proportion to fragile body. Associated colour description: Carapace pale pink to red or orange. Pereopods vividly striped in red and white. Associated size description: Carapace up to 15 mm length; legs up to 150 mm. Associated distribution: Pacific to Mozambique, Madagascar and South Africa. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Whole-specimen reference image for Maja cornuta (Agulhas spider crab) from the species identification page. PDF page 200, printed page 197. Associated diagnostic features: Carapace pear-shaped, behind the post-ocular tooth four large marginal teeth, followed by one small submarginal tooth on hind part of branchial region, in the middle line three prominent spines on gastric region, rest of surface with smaller scattered spines, a pair of short spines on hind margin. Cheliped with granules on merus and carpus, fingers gaping at base in full-grown males. Body often camouflaged with attached animals. Previously called Maja capensis. Associated colour description: Yellow brown to orange-red or carmine. Associated size description: Up to 100-150 mm carapace width. Associated distribution: South Coast of South Africa, from False Bay to Durban; 10-60 m depth. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Specimen detail reference image for Pyromaia tuberculata (Tuberculate pear crab) from the species identification page. PDF page 201, printed page 198. Associated diagnostic features: Carapace pear-shaped and convex, with four large tubercles, one anteriorly central and three larger ones posteriorly, each covered in small knobbly projections. Rostrum pointed, curved spine behind eye. Chelae inflated in male (shown), much more slender in female. Pereopods slender and elongate, with long dactyls. Associated colour description: Off-white with light brown mottled areas. Associated size description: Carapace width up to 15-20 mm. Associated distribution: Native range is Pacific North America. Potentially introduced species to South Africa. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Specimen detail reference image for Rochinia hertwigi (rochinia Sunday/ Two-prong crab) from the species identification page. PDF page 202, printed page 199. Associated diagnostic features: Carapace with several distinctive flat-topped projecting tubercles. Male (left) with larger chelipeds than female (right). Carapace pear- shaped, produced anteriorly into distinctive rostrum composed of two slender, long, tapering spines, separated at their bases. Flat-topped tubercles often not well-developed, or hidden by bulbous swellings in posterior lateral regions. Chelipeds and pereopods long and slender. Associated colour description: Pale orange – frequently covered in mud, hydroids and tunicates, etc. Associated size description: Male length up to 63 mm, female up to 43 mm. Associated distribution: West and South Coasts of South Africa. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Specimen detail reference image for Exodromidia spinosissima (horned baboon crab) from the species identification page. PDF page 203, printed page 200. Associated diagnostic features: Carapace roundly elongate, with two prominent, divergent spiniform processes projecting from front of carapace. Chelipeds long with strong chelae. Last two pereopods modified to be “carrier” legs folded behind carapace. Covered with short stiff hairs, longer bristles towards edges of carapace, chelipeds and pereopods. Chelipeds larger in males than females. Associated colour description: Marbled orange to brick-red with white. Associated size description: Up to 18 mm carapace diameter, 20 mm length. Associated distribution: Endemic. West Coast of South Africa to Agulhas Bank. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Specimen detail reference image for Exodromidia spinosa (Furry baboon crab) from the species identification page. PDF page 204, printed page 201. Associated diagnostic features: Rounded crab, especially when chelipeds are held close to body. Often covered in mud and sand and looks like a stone. Carapace convex, mottled orange to red, covered with fine short hairs and with scattered small, conical tubercles dorsally. Frontal lobes large and triangular. Lateral margin with three distinct teeth. Male chelipeds much larger and longer than female; female chelipeds often tucked under carapace. Last two pairs of pereopods reduced and folded behind carapace, occasionally used to carry sponges or ascidians. Associated colour description: Bright orange/brick-red, mottled with white. Tips of chelipeds white. Associated size description: Male carapace width up to 34-35 mm, female 22-25 mm. Associated distribution: Southern African endemic. Mainly West Coast of South Africa. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

Lateral margin with three distinct teeth. Male chelipeds much larger and longer than female; female chelipeds often tucked under carapace. Last two pairs of pereopods reduced and folded behind carapace, occasionally used to carry sponges or ascidians. Associated colour description: Bright orange/brick-red, mottled with white. Tips of chelipeds white. Associated size description: Male carapace width up to 34-35 mm, female 22-25 mm. Associated distribution: Southern African endemic. Mainly West Coast of South Africa. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Specimen detail reference image for Dromidia aegibotus (Sponge crab) from the species identification page. PDF page 205, printed page 202. Associated diagnostic features: Fairly large, furry crab with domed carapace; four teeth on either side of front margin of carapace. Body and pereopods covered with dense brown coating of short hairs. Last two pairs of pereopods shortened and bent back over carapace, may carry sponge. Associated colour description: Red with brown, mud-covered hairy layer. Associated size description: Carapace up to 80-90 mm wide. Associated distribution: Endemic. South Coast of South Africa. content: Specimen detail reference image for Dromidia hirsutissima (Shaggy sponge crab) from the species identification page. PDF page 206, printed page 203. Associated diagnostic features: Body covered with short, stiff pile and long, dense, fibrous and shaggy brown or yellow hairs. Carapace broader than long, with several teeth on front margin and one on lateral margin. Fifth pereopods not markedly shorter in length than fourth, but more slender and folded back over carapace. Typically carry a cloak of sponge or ascidian over carapace (photo on right). Associated colour description: Muddy brown, orange or yellow. Tips of chelipeds white. Associated size description: Up to 55 mm carapace width. Associated distribution: Endemic. West and South Coasts of South Africa. Extracted image dimensions: 192x192.

content: Specimen detail reference image for Speodromia platyarthrodes (Boxer/Muscle crab) from the species identification page. PDF page 207, printed page 204. Associated diagnostic features: Upper surface of carapace inflated into three large humps, surface studded with minute sessile, scale- like setae; frontal margin with rounded projection. Undersurface of carapace bearing unusual deep cavity thought to be associated with respiration; closed anteriorly by cheliped and posteriorly by pereopods 2-4. Associated colour description: Orange mottled with paler areas. Associated size description: Up to 38 mm carapace width. Associated distribution: Endemic. South Coast of South Africa. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Specimen detail reference image for Pseudodromia rotunda (rounded sponge crab) from the species identification page. PDF page 208, printed page 205. Associated diagnostic features: Characterised by rounded, strongly convex carapace and unusualy elongate last pair of pereopods folded upwards to hold ascidian cloak. Two upper frontal teeth are slightly divergent, allowing the lower median tooth to be seen in dorsal view. Associated colour description: Crab inside ascidian is pale orange to peach in colour. Associated size description: Up to 40 mm carapace width. Associated distribution: Saldanha to Southern Mozambique, predominantly South Coast of South Africa. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Specimen detail reference image for Pseudodromia spp. (cloaked ascidian crab) from the species identification page. PDF page 209, printed page 206. Associated diagnostic features: Crab almost fully enclosed by ascidian growing on dorsal carapace. Tips of chelipeds usually red and white in colour. Associated colour description: Diverse range of colour, shapes and texture of ascidian coating the dorsal carapace. Crab usually muddy brown but pale orange, crimson or rose red when cleaned and abdomen more or less mottled or speckled. Associated size description: Usually between 20-40 mm carapace width. Associated distribution: Predominantly South Coast of South Africa. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Specimen detail reference image for Homola barbata (Periscope eye crab) from the species identification page. PDF page 210, printed page 207. Associated diagnostic features: Carapace squarish, longer than wide, prominent spines covering frontal portion of dorsal carapace, with two lateral spines projecting from front corners. Covered in short, fine orange hairs. Eyestalks long and eyes large. Last pereopods modified to fold back over carapace and often carries sponge over back. Often covered in mud. Associated colour description: Orange with paler speckles. Associated size description: Usually between 16-28 mm carapace width (male), and 22 mm carapace length (ovigerous female). Associated distribution: South Coast of South Africa; depth 10-679 m. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

Associated colour description: Orange with paler speckles. Associated size description: Usually between 16-28 mm carapace width (male), and 22 mm carapace length (ovigerous female). Associated distribution: South Coast of South Africa; depth 10-679 m. Extracted image dimensions: 156x141.

content: Specimen detail reference image for Nautilocorystes ocellatus (ringed porcelain crab) from the species identification page. PDF page 211, printed page 208. Associated diagnostic features: Easily recognised by the unusual elongated carapace with its rounded front armed with four sharp teeth on either side. Antennae elongated and held together by interlocking hairs to form a tube, down which water is drawn while the crab is buried in the sand. Associated colour description: Light brown-orange, with thin reddish-brown lines forming four circlular patches on carapace. Associated size description: Up to 34 mm length in males (28 mm in females) and 24-38 mm width. Associated distribution: South Coast of South Africa and South-West Africa northward to Angola and off the Congo; depths 0-82 m. Burrows in sand. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Specimen detail reference image for Miersiograpsus kingsleyi (Orange hairy sponge crab) from the species identification page. PDF page 212, printed page 209. Associated diagnostic features: Small crab often co-occurring with sponges. Carapace square, front margin bilobed from dorsal view, distinct tooth outside eye and another on side of carapace. Eyestalks covered in fine hairs. Pereopods covered in bristly hairs, chelae strong, lower margin of merus serrated. Associated colour description: Pale orange to yellowish, with pale amber hairs. Associated size description: Carapace width no more than 15 mm. Associated distribution: West Coast of South Africa to KwaZulu-Natal. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Specimen detail reference image for Neopilumnoplax heterochir (Smooth choc-tip/ Smooth dark fingered crab) from the species identification page. PDF page 213, printed page 210. Associated diagnostic features: Relatively smooth orange-golden to brown carapace with well-defined epibranchial ridges (ridge on mid- lateral dorsal carapace). Frontal margin (rostrum) straight, with three fairly large lateral teeth behind eyes, second two pronounced and curved. Chelipeds subequal, left side slightly larger, upper surface granulate, claw fingers dark brown or black. Row of knobs along upper edge of pereopods. Associated colour description: Golden brown to orange, with brown to black fingertips of cheliped. Pereopods orange with pale white bands. Associated size description: Up to 35-40 mm carapace width. Associated distribution: West and South Coasts of South Africa, extending to East London. Reported from 137-710 m. Extracted image dimensions: 192x192.

content: Specimen detail reference image for Monodaeus sp. (Furrowed brow choc-tip crab) from the species identification page. PDF page 214, printed page 211. Associated diagnostic features: Carapace orange- to red-speckled, nodular and marked with distinct grooves running back from anterior edge. Four blunt spines projecting from lateral edge to just before eye, posterior spines have white tips. Rostrum square with no projections. Pereopods hairy and frequently coated in mud. Chelae subequal, ends of finger and hand black. Associated colour description: Orange-red-brown speckled, with paler portions of body, fingers black. Associated size description: Usually ± 40 mm diameter carapace width. Associated distribution: West and South Coasts of South Africa, extending into West Africa. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Specimen detail reference image for Chaceon chuni (red crab) from the species identification page. PDF page 215, printed page 212. Associated diagnostic features: Large orange crab, sometimes with black mottled carapace. Carapace quadrangular, smooth, lateral margin with five teeth on each side of eye, second and fourth smaller. Pereopods long and unmodified. Dactyls of fifth pereopod laterally flattened (from sides). Commonly caught in trawl nets in large numbers (> 100). Associated colour description: Bright orange, sometimes with black mottled colouration. Associated size description: Average 80 mm width, 68 mm length. Maximum recorded: 138 mm width, 122 mm length. Associated distribution: West and South Coasts of South Africa between 300-1 400 m depth. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Specimen detail reference image for Chaceon macphersoni (White-leg crab) from the species identification page. PDF page 216, printed page 213. Associated diagnostic features: Large crab, often co-occurring with Chaceon chuni in trawl catches. Clearly distinguished by the white pereopods with orange tips and orange blotches of shading on carapace. Carapace is granular in texture and has characteristic markings. Lateral margin with five teeth on each side of eye, second and fourth smaller. Tips of pereopods are dorso- ventrally flattened (from top to bottom). Associated colour description: White pereopods with orange tips and orange shading on carapace, no orange tips on chelae. Associated size description: Average 80 mm carapace width (on average larger than C. chuni), but recorded up to 150 mm carapace width. Associated distribution: Southern African endemic. West and South Coasts of South Africa; 250-900 m depth. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

PDF page 216, printed page 213. Associated diagnostic features: Large crab, often co-occurring with Chaceon chuni in trawl catches. Clearly distinguished by the white pereopods with orange tips and orange blotches of shading on carapace. Carapace is granular in texture and has characteristic markings. Lateral margin with five teeth on each side of eye, second and fourth smaller. Tips of pereopods are dorso- ventrally flattened (from top to bottom). Associated colour description: White pereopods with orange tips and orange shading on carapace, no orange tips on chelae. Associated size description: Average 80 mm carapace width (on average larger than C. chuni), but recorded up to 150 mm carapace width. Associated distribution: Southern African endemic. West and South Coasts of South Africa; 250-900 m depth. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Whole-specimen reference image for Chaceon maritae (Northern/Deep-sea red crab) from the species identification page. PDF page 217, printed page 214. Associated diagnostic features: Similar in appearance to other Chaceon species, having five teeth on each side of eye, second and fourth smaller or obsolete. Tips of pereopods are dorso-ventrally flattened (from top to bottom). Carapace can be granulated in frontal portion. Associated colour description: Pale orange to yellow. Associated size description: Average carapace width 95 mm, reported up to 131 mm. Associated distribution: From Agulhas Bank along Atlantic coast into North- West Africa forming part of an important fishery; between 100 and 900+ m depth. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Specimen detail reference image for Macropipus australis (Painted swimming crab) from the species identification page. PDF page 218, printed page 215. Associated diagnostic features: Carapace with three frontal and four sharp lateral spines, and one lateral spine projecting horizontally. Has distinct symmetrical white markings against deep red colouration, giving a painted appearance. Fifth pair of pereopods modified as swimming paddles. Associated colour description: Brick red to maroon colouration with white markings. Associated size description: Average 60-70 mm carapace width. Associated distribution: West Coast of South Africa extending northwards to Namibia and Angola. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Specimen detail reference image for Ovalipes iridescens (Iridescent swimming crab) from the species identification page. PDF page 219, printed page 216. Associated diagnostic features: Carapace with three sharp teeth between eyes, broad orbital notch and then five lateral teeth to side, dorsal surface finely granulated except two paler oval membranous areas posteriorly. Chelipeds two to five distinct spines on upper surface. Last pair of pereopods modified for swimming. Associated colour description: Red markings on paler yellowish background, chelae white-tipped. Iridescent, particularly on chelipeds and carapace. Associated size description: Up to 80 mm carapace width. Associated distribution: South and East Coasts of South Africa, to eastern Pacific. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Specimen detail reference image for Ovalipes trimaculatus (Three-spot swimming crab) from the species identification page. PDF page 220, printed page 217. Associated diagnostic features: Carapace pink, finely granulate, front with four teeth between eyes, a tooth on upper orbital margin and four strong teeth on antero-lateral margin behind outer orbital tooth, three distinctive red marks – a central curved mark and two dots on posterior corners. Last pair of pereopods modified for swimming. Formerly known as Ovalipes punctatus. Associated colour description: Creamy grey or pale buff, speckled with reddish dots, a median crescentric red mark and an oval red spot near each postero-lateral corner. Associated size description: Up to 80-100 mm carapace width. Associated distribution: West and South Coasts of South Africa (and widespread around Southern Hemisphere). Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Specimen detail reference image for Bathynectes piperitus (red and white legged swimming crab) from the species identification page. PDF page 221, printed page 218. Associated diagnostic features: Carapace oval, with scalloped ridge running horizontally across centre. Four rounded projections across front of carapace. Four spines on anterior margin of each side, then one very elongated and pointed spine projecting from each side. Fifth pair of pereopods modified as swimming paddles. Associated colour description: Orange carapace with distinct red and white banded legs. Associated size description: Carapace width between 15-86 mm (including lateral spine). Associated distribution: West Coast of South Africa; 200-628 m depths. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Specimen detail reference image for Charybdis smithii (Smith's swimming crab) from the species identification page. PDF page 222, printed page 219. Associated diagnostic features: Carapace smooth, front edge with four pairs of short teeth, sides with four broad, peg-like marginal teeth and a single pointed tooth. Outstretched chelipeds easily double carapace width. Chelipeds with five to six longitudinal rows of tubercles. Associated colour description: Mottled reddish-brown. Associated size description: Up to 120 mm carapace width. Associated distribution: South and East Coasts of South Africa, aggregate in upper 150 m layer, sometimes in large densities where they can be important prey for epipelagic predators. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Specimen detail reference image for Atelecyclus rotundatus (round sand crab/Old man's face crab) from the species identification page. PDF page 223, printed page 220. Associated diagnostic features: Carapace rounded, surface granular, thickly setose (with bristles) around margins and anteriorly around mouthparts; tridentate between eyes, lateral margin with about 10 serrate teeth. Chelipeds equal, large and powerful, held closely up against front of body, strongly setose dorsally. Chela with horizontal lines of granules, fingers darker. Pereopods short, setose around margins and granular. Associated colour description: Pinkish brown, chela with darker fingers. Associated size description: Carapace width up to 30 mm. Associated distribution: West Coast, Saldanha Bay to South Coast of South Africa, Port Elizabeth and widespread through North and South Atlantic. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Specimen detail reference image for Mursia cristiata (red spotted crab/ Masked crab) from the species identification page. PDF page 224, printed page 221. Associated diagnostic features: Carapace roughly oval, pale orange with bright red tubercles. Front lateral edges of carapace crenulate, with about 10 small teeth, followed by a much larger, sharp spine projecting laterally. Chelipeds broad and strongly spinose, mostly held close to the mouth, hence the name ‘masked’ crab. Associated colour description: Pale orange with red tubercles. Associated size description: Carapace width up to 40 mm. Associated distribution: West and South Coasts of South Africa, extending to Durban. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Specimen detail reference image for Goneplax clevai (Angular/Waveline crab) from the species identification page. PDF page 225, printed page 222. Associated diagnostic features: Smooth, quadrangular carapace, wider than long and with two strong forward-directed lateral teeth. Colour pattern distinctive, marked with distinct scalloped line approximately midway across carapace. Front portion of carapace darker brown, rear half lighter brown. Pereopods long, male has much longer chelipeds than female (female depicted). Previously known as Gonoplax rhomboides, but South African material described as distinct new species by Guinot and Castro (2007). Associated colour description: Red and orange – darker red patterned line across carapace, posterior part of carapace and pereopods paler. Associated size description: Usually between 30-50 mm carapace width. Associated distribution: West Coast of South Africa to KwaZulu-Natal. Extracted image dimensions: 192x191.

content: Specimen detail reference image for Carcinoplax longimanus (Long-arm pebble crab) from the species identification page. PDF page 226, printed page 223. Associated diagnostic features: Carapace rounded, smooth, antero-lateral margin with two slight knobs behind outer orbital tooth. Chelipeds vary in length with gender and age, but extremely elongate in adult males (see photo). Palm with a distinct rounded tubercle on inner surface. Associated colour description: Buff or pale salmon. Associated size description: Usually between 50-60 mm carapace width. Associated distribution: South Coast of South Africa, extending up East Coast into Mozambique. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Specimen detail reference image for Afrophila punctata (Pebble crab) from the species identification page. PDF page 227, printed page 224. Associated diagnostic features: Carapace oval and swollen, surface finely granulate. Eyes small. Pereopods short and weak. Chelae robust, equal and elongate, especially in males. Previously known as Philyra punctata. Associated colour description: Off-white. Associated size description: Carapace width up to 16 mm in female, 21 mm in male. Associated distribution: Saldanha to Algoa Bay, South Africa. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Whole-specimen reference image for Ebalia tuberculosa (Speckled orange crab) from the species identification page. PDF page 228, printed page 225. Associated diagnostic features: Very small species. Carapace rounded-quadrangular with pair of tiny projections on posterior lateral edges. Carapace with distinctive fine red to orange speckles over entire surface, extending in patches onto legs and claws. Chelae elongate, merus cylindrical in cross section, chelipeds with powerful chelae. Associated colour description: Mottled orange to white. Associated size description: Carapace width between 5-15 mm. Associated distribution: West, South and East Coasts of South Africa. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Whole-specimen reference image for Tanaoa pustulosus (Tail spike crab) from the species identification page. PDF page 229, printed page 226. Associated diagnostic features: Carapace rounded in dorsal view, surface covered in fine granules. Two small triangular projections above eyes and five small, evenly spaced granular projections around lateral margin of carapace. Posterior margin with one pair of larger tubercles ventrally, above which lies a distinctive sharply pointed and upturned spike. Chelae elongate with narrow claw. Associated colour description: Orange to red. Associated size description: Carapace width usually about 34 mm in adults. content: Specimen detail reference image for Hornera erugata (Brittle tree bryozoan) from the species identification page. PDF page 232, printed page 229. Associated diagnostic features: Erect, delicately calcified and branching. Forms curved tree-like branches with secondary branches emanating from a central tubular main branch. Tubular zooids protrude from the frontal surface (usually facing away from substratum), while the basal surface is smooth in this species. Commonly epizoic on other bryozoans and hard substrata. Associated colour description: Off-white. Associated size description: Branches may be 50-100 mm in length. Associated distribution: Endemic. Occur at depths of 35-90 m on the West, South and East Coasts of South Africa. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Specimen detail reference image for Alcyonidium rhomboidale (rubbery bryozoan) from the species identification page. PDF page 233, printed page 230. content: Whole-specimen reference image for Flustramorpha marginata (Green strappy-tree bryozoan) from the species identification page. PDF page 234, printed page 231. content: Specimen detail reference image for Securiflustra sp. 1 (Paper tree bryozoan) from the species identification page. PDF page 236, printed page 233. Associated diagnostic features: Erect, forming lightly calcified, flexible, narrow, strappy fronds that branch dichotomously, having paper-thin blades that are yellow to brown in colour. Attach to substrate by a holdfast. Associated colour description: Yellow to brown. Associated size description: Branches may be 50-100 mm in length. Associated distribution: Recorded from the South Coast of South Africa at a depth of 72 m but may have greater depth range. The South African specimens appear to be consistent with the genus Securiflustra, which is reported to be endemic to Europe. Taxonomy of this species is uncertain and specimens must be retained. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Specimen detail reference image for Turbicellepora valligera (False stag-horn bryozoan) from the species identification page. PDF page 241, printed page 238. Associated diagnostic features: Erect, but originates from an encrusting base which develops into tapered cylindrical branches that are heavily calcified and branch dichotomously. Resembles stag-horn coral. Associated colour description: Off-white to light orange, but sometimes with a green tinge. Associated size description: Branches may be 50-100 mm in length. Associated distribution: Endemic. West Coast, Port Nolloth to the East Coast of South Africa. Depth range from 2 to 278 m. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Specimen detail reference image for Adeonella spp. (Sabre bryozoan) from the species identification page. PDF page 242, printed page 239. Associated diagnostic features: Erect, brittle, forming calcified, flattened, strap-like colonies that branch dichotomously. Branches may fuse to form coral-like structures. Zooids are large enough to be visible on both sides of straps. Often mistaken for Stylaster hydrozoans. Not flexible and has sandpapery texture. Associated colour description: Mainly white, but some species may be tan to light brown in colour. Associated size description: Colonies may be anything from 50-200 mm in length. Associated distribution: Most species endemic to South Africa. Found in waters of the West, South and East Coasts of South Africa. Depth range from shallow subtidal to 880m. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Whole-specimen reference image for Chaperiopsis multifida (Furry bryozoan) from the species identification page. PDF page 244, printed page 241. Associated diagnostic features: Erect, but originates from an encrusting base which develops into a series of folded erect plates. Zooids are found on both sides. Colony appears furry on the surface because of several branched spines that cover zooids. Associated colour description: Dark red to maroon or dusky pink. Associated size description: Colonies may be 100-150 mm in diameter. Associated distribution: Endemic to South Africa. West Coast of South Africa to East Coast, Durban from shallow subtidal to 375 m. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

Associated colour description: Off-white or cream to orange. Associated size description: Colonies may be 50–200 mm in diameter. Associated distribution: Found between depths of 2-775 m on the West, South and East Coasts of South Africa. Extracted image dimensions: 156x141.

content: Distribution map for Megerlina capensis (Common name ribbed Lamp shell) from the species identification page. PDF page 250, printed page 247. Associated diagnostic features: Small rounded sub-pentagonal to sub-quadrate shells with length and width about equal. Ventral valve (shell) slightly deeper than dorsal valve. Anterior commissure (line along which the valves meet, viewed from the anterior) gently sulcate (i.e. with a broad U-shaped deflection). Relatively large pedicle opening bounded laterally by small, flat, triangular inter-areas. Fine concentric growth lines and 24-33 rounded radial ribs visible exteriorly from the 5-mm growth stage. Associated colour description: Usually pinkish or reddish but may be white or cream, sometimes with red margins. Associated size description: Usually not more than 15 mm in length. Associated distribution: West, South and East Coasts of South Africa. Extracted image dimensions: 156x141.

content: Whole-specimen reference image for Xenobrochus sp. (Smooth Lamp shell) from the species identification page. PDF page 251, printed page 248. Associated diagnostic features: Small, elongate oval, strongly biconvex shells. Ventral valve (shell) deeper than dorsal valve. Anterior commissure (line along which the valves meet, viewed from the anterior) straight (rectimarginate). Pedicle opening small, sub-circular. Shell surface smooth except for fine concentric growth lines. Shell material very thin. Associated colour description: White. Associated size description: Usually around 11 or 12 mm in length. Associated distribution: West, South and East coasts of South Africa. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Distribution map for Calliotropis granolirata (cape cog shell) from the species identification page. PDF page 256, printed page 253. Associated diagnostic features: Shell small, with conical spire and rounded base; sculptured by strong spiral cords bearing well- developed granules; spire whorls with three cords above and including periphery; base with four cords; umbilicus closed; aperture nacreous (mother-of- pearl) when fresh. Associated colour description: Uniformly milky-white to pale buff, lustreless. Associated size description: Length (height) up to 13 mm. Associated distribution: South African endemic. To date known reliably only from deep water off the Cape Agulhas–Cape Point region, to depths of 2 750 m. More accurate locality data is urgently needed. Extracted image dimensions: 156x141.

content: Whole-specimen reference image for Calliostoma perfragile (Agulhas calliostoma) from the species identification page. PDF page 257, printed page 254. Associated diagnostic features: Shell top-shaped, with conical spire and somewhat flattened base; spire whorls slightly convex, suture shallowly indented; periphery roundly angular, but not keeled; sculptured by spiral cords of which the first two to three below suture are finely granular, the others smooth; cord intervals often with a fine spiral thread; base smoother with several broad spiral cords around umbilical region; umbilicus closed; aperture nacreous (mother-of-pearl); operculum circular, multi-spiral. Associated colour description: Spire overall pale orange-brown (biscuit-coloured), rather glossy and slightly iridescent; under microscope spiral cords whitish, their intervals orange-brown; periphery with a spiral row of dash- like brown markings; base paler. Associated size description: Length up to 25 mm. Associated distribution: South African endemic. Agulhas Bank (Cape canyon to southern Transkei), perhaps also KwaZulu-Natal, 100-350 m. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Specimen detail reference image for Turritella declivis (Zebra turret shell/ Bokhoring) from the species identification page. PDF page 258, printed page 255. Associated diagnostic features: Shell long and slender, whorls flattened or slightly concave (hollowed inwards); base of last whorl angular; aperture small and slightly flaring at base; surface with fine, curved axial growth-lines, becoming obsolete on lower part of each whorl; no spiral sculpture; outer lip thin, often damaged. Associated colour description: Shell cream-coloured with a broad brown mid-whorl spiral band; shell surface sometimes etched and colour indistinct; juveniles with brown spots below suture. Associated size description: Length up to 100 mm, but usually less than 65 mm. Associated distribution: South African endemic. Common on the Agulhas Bank (Kei River to False Bay), in places hugely abundant and dominating the marine benthos; also found on West Coast, but evidently in much lower numbers (more specimens needed to confirm its distribution on West Coast). Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Whole-specimen reference image for Turritella ferruginea (Speckled turret shell) from the species identification page. PDF page 259, printed page 256. Associated diagnostic features: Shell relatively large, many-whorled, long and slender, tapering gradually toward apex; whorls slightly convex (rounded outward), sculptured with numerous close-set, crisp, spiral threads; surface dull; basal angle distinct, delineated by a stronger spiral cord (arrowed in figure); aperture rounded; outer lip distinctly concave (hollowed inwards). Associated colour description: Cream to buff, speckled with reddish-brown, sometimes in the form of curved axial flames. Associated size description: Length up to 110 mm, occasionally more. Associated distribution: South African endemic. Agulhas Bank (False Bay to Algoa Bay), 40–210 m. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Specimen detail reference image for Turritella sanguinea (Mottled turret shell) from the species identification page. PDF page 260, printed page 257. Associated diagnostic features: Shell relatively large, many-whorled, long and slender, tapering gradually toward apex; whorls convex (rounded outward), sculptured with relatively uniform rounded or flat-topped spiral cords; surface dull; basal angle not delineated by a stronger spiral cord; aperture rounded; outer lip shallowly concave (hollowed inwards). Associated colour description: Cream to buff with reddish-brown dashes on the spiral cords, sometimes aligned into axial flames or bands. Associated size description: Length up to 100 mm, occasionally more. Associated distribution: South African endemic. Agulhas Bank (False Bay to East London) and extending northwards into KwaZulu- Natal (the smaller T. salisburyi form), 30–120 m. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Distribution map for Cypraeovula iutsui (Globular cape cowrie) from the species identification page. PDF page 261, printed page 258. Associated diagnostic features: Shell globular, often almost spherical, spire entirely enveloped by last adult whorl; aperture elongate with a thickened, white, denticulate margin; teeth on outer lip (labrum) stronger, numbering 17–25; juveniles (‘bulla’ stage) common, retaining vestiges of spire and narrowed siphonal region. Associated colour description: West coast specimens vary from opaque white to pale plum with few dorsal markings; in Agulhas Bank specimens the dorsum is more densely patterned with reddish-brown spots and blotches. Associated size description: Adult shell length 22–41 mm. Associated distribution: South African endemic. West coast to South coast, Agulhas Bank; from Olifants River Mouth to Port Alfred, 50–350 m. Extracted image dimensions: 156x141.

content: Distribution map for Triviella spp. (Smooth pearl cowries) from the species identification page. PDF page 262, printed page 259. Associated diagnostic features: The genus Triviella (previously treated as a subgenus of Trivia) consists of several species that are very similar and require microscopic examination of the live animal for accurate species-level identification. The shells of smooth pearl cowrie species are inflated and globular, with a thickened labrum (outer lip of aperture), bearing well-developed denticles that continue as transverse ridges around the outer lip, sometimes extending onto lower lateral part of dorsum; inner lip of aperture also denticulate. Associated colour description: Shell uniformly white to rose-pink or plum; mantle colour highly variable with mottled, blotched, spotted and reticulate patterns, often matching that of the tunicates on which they feed. Associated size description: Length ranges from 11 mm to 27 mm, depending on species. Associated distribution: South African endemic. Agulhas Bank, from the Atlantic coast of the Cape Peninsula to the Transkei region, shallow subtidal to 160 m. Extracted image dimensions: 156x141.

content: Specimen detail reference image for Lamellaria/Coriocella - (Velutinid) from the species identification page. PDF page 263, printed page 260. Associated diagnostic features: Resembles a dorid nudibranch sea slug, but anatomically quite different. Shell present, but completely internal, covered by fleshy mantle; ventral surface with a distinct foot and head bearing tentacles with basal eyes; anterior of notum (dorsal surface) indented in mid-line, forming a short siphon; mantle relatively firm, but texture somewhat gelatinous, for the most part smooth; internal shell ear-like, thin and fragile. Associated colour description: Translucent, greyish-white to pinkish or yellow with black/brown spots and blotches. Colouration variable, resembling that of the ascidian prey on which they live and feed and thus providing camouflage. Associated size description: Length 25–40 mm. Associated distribution: Common on West coast and Agulhas Bank. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Specimen detail reference image for Euspira napus (Moon shell) from the species identification page. PDF page 264, printed page 261. Associated diagnostic features: Shell rounded, solid and smooth, with a low spire; aperture semi-circular with a thin outer lip; base with a distinct, but narrow umbilicus and a somewhat thickened edge to the inner lip; sculpture comprises only fine, close-set growth-lines. Living animal with a horny operculum. Associated colour description: Shell white; periostracum (thin outer skin-like covering) dull brown, usually with a pattern of fine spiral lines. Associated size description: Diameter 30–40 mm. Associated distribution: South African endemic. Agulhas Bank (False Bay to western Transkei), 50–210 m. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Specimen detail reference image for Semicassis labiata (helmet/Lipped bonnet shell) from the species identification page. PDF page 265, printed page 262. Associated diagnostic features: Shell rounded with a low, rather pointed spire; glossy and smooth, but usually with one to two rows of low nodules in shoulder region; outer lip thickened in adult specimens; anterior end with a pronounced, up-curving siphonal notch. Very variable in size, strength of nodules, shell thickness and depth of colouration. Agulhas Bank shells usually larger, thinner, with weak nodules and less vivid colouration. Associated colour description: Pale pinkish-brown to yellowish-brown, some specimens with three to five rows of diffuse semi- circular whitish spots; outer lip with deep purple blotches, frequently in pairs. Shell colours fade noticeably after death. Associated size description: Length up to 80 mm. Associated distribution: West coast False Bay to KwaZulu-Natal north coast, subtidal to 150 m. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Whole-specimen reference image for Eudolium bairdii (Baird's bonnet shell) from the species identification page. PDF page 266, printed page 263. Associated diagnostic features: Shell thin, globose, spire prominent with rounded whorls and strongly indented suture; sculpture of well-defined, narrow spiral cords of alternating strength; outer lip thickened and flaring outward in adult specimens, its inner edge finely toothed; anterior end with a pronounced siphonal notch. Associated colour description: Shell buff to pale brown, the primary spiral cords darker brown; spire may have a grey-blue tinge; tip of spire (protoconch/apex), if present, clearly distinct and brown in colour. Associated size description: Adult shell length 40–65 mm. Associated distribution: Widely distributed in many parts of the world; recorded off South and East coast of South Africa, 100–500 m. Extracted image dimensions: 396x192.

content: Specimen detail reference image for Tonna dunkeri (Boxing-glove shell) from the species identification page. PDF page 267, printed page 264. Associated diagnostic features: Moderate to large, fragile shells, globular in shape with a very large aperture and low spire; sculptured by well-developed, broad, flat-topped, spiral cords; base with a pronounced siphonal notch. Adult animals lack an operculum. A variable species with shallow- and deep-water forms. On the Agulhas Bank the shell is more globular and has a lower spire with a strong shoulder and somewhat sunken suture. Associated colour description: Fresh shells light brown to orange-brown, ribs marked with irregular white blotches, bordered by darker brown bars. Associated size description: Shell length up to 125 mm. Associated distribution: South African endemic. South coast Agulhas Bank and East coast, 50–100 m. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

Adult animals lack an operculum. A variable species with shallow- and deep-water forms. On the Agulhas Bank the shell is more globular and has a lower spire with a strong shoulder and somewhat sunken suture. Associated colour description: Fresh shells light brown to orange-brown, ribs marked with irregular white blotches, bordered by darker brown bars. Associated size description: Shell length up to 125 mm. Associated distribution: South African endemic. South coast Agulhas Bank and East coast, 50–100 m. Extracted image dimensions: 156x141.

content: Specimen detail reference image for Charonia lampas (Pink lady) from the species identification page. PDF page 268, printed page 265. Associated diagnostic features: Shell large to very large, robust with a distinct shoulder bearing strong, rounded knobs; sculptured elsewhere by rather flat spiral cords of varying strength, strongest on base, with numerous finer intermediary threads; growth varices usually present on spire whorls; inner lip glossy, reflected over columella (inner lip) and bearing distinct ridges; additional ridges on parietal region, that closest to insertion of outer lip particularly strong; outer lip thickened with ridge-like teeth, often arranged in sets of two or three; siphonal notch well-developed. Associated colour description: Buff to pinkish-brown, dotted, mottled and blotched with shades of brown to purplish-brown; base of inner lip and teeth of outer lip dark purple-brown, their intervals whitish. Foot of living animal orange- pink, often with white spots; tentacles orange and usually with black barring. Associated size description: Length up to 290 mm. Associated distribution: False Bay to Kosi Bay, subtidally to 100 m, rarely more. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Specimen detail reference image for Fusitriton magellanicus (Waffle whelk) from the species identification page. PDF page 269, printed page 266. Associated diagnostic features: Shell broadly spindle-shaped, relatively light in weight; sculpture reticulate (cross-hatched), nodular at intersections, strongest on spire, often weaker on last adult whorl; spire sometimes with distinct growth varices (arrowed in photo), but these sometimes weak or absent; aperture large, its base extending as a somewhat sinuous siphonal canal of moderate length. Associated colour description: Shell white, occasionally with pinkish spiral ridges; surface of living specimens covered with bristly, light brown periostracum; bristles conspicuous in juvenile shells, arranged in spiral pattern. Associated size description: Largest sampled specimen 145 mm in length, but usually smaller than this. Associated distribution: South African endemic. Agulhas Bank and throughout West coast region, 50–550 m. The most common whelk species occurring on West coast. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Specimen detail reference image for Afrocominella capensis simoniana (Variable Agulhas whelk) from the species identification page. PDF page 270, printed page 267. Associated diagnostic features: Shell spindle-shaped to biconic (two cones), proportions variable; deep-water specimens less elongate; whorls shouldered with sculpture of distinct spiral cords, also with low axial ribs in shoulder region, rendering shoulder somewhat nodular; outer lip thickened and internally ridged at maturity; siphonal canal short. Associated colour description: Cream, greyish-white or fawn, with orange or reddish-brown markings (mottled, spirally banded or with axial flames); aperture generally white in deep-water specimens. Associated size description: Length up to 40 mm, shallow-water form longer. Associated distribution: South African endemic. Agulhas Bank, subtidal to 160 m. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Specimen detail reference image for Africolaria rutila (Smooth horse conch) from the species identification page. PDF page 271, printed page 268. Associated diagnostic features: Shell spindle-shaped, spire and aperture of similar length; whorls evenly rounded; sculptured by fine spiral threads; siphonal canal of moderate length; inner lip with one spiral columella pleat at start of siphonal canal, occasionally a second one adjacent to this; parietal region with an indistinct, in-running, spiral ridge just below insertion of outer lip; interior of outer lip smooth; tip of spire slightly bulbous when not damaged or worn. Associated colour description: Whitish with a thin, pale horn-coloured or orange- brown periostracum, often eroded on spire. Animal yellowish-white to pale yellow. Associated size description: Length up to 175 mm, perhaps more. Associated distribution: South African endemic. West coast to Namibian border and Agulhas Bank, 65–500 m. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

content: Specimen detail reference image for Africolaria thersites (Varicose horse conch) from the species identification page. PDF page 272, printed page 269. Associated diagnostic features: Shell spindle-shaped, spire half to two-thirds total length of aperture; whorls usually with strong, widely spaced axial ribs (strongest at shoulder), but sculpture variable and some specimens with virtually no ribs on later whorls; spiral sculpture of very fine threads; siphonal canal of moderate length; inner lip with a strong spiral columella pleat at start of siphonal canal, a second weaker one just above this; a third narrow, in-running, spiral ridge in parietal region, below insertion of outer lip; interior of outer lip smooth; tip of spire slightly bulbous when not damaged or worn. Associated colour description: Shell white with a thin, pale horn-brown periostracum, often eroded on spire. Associated size description: Length up to 100 mm. Associated distribution: South African endemic. Agulhas Bank (west of Cape Town to Tsitsikamma), 100–200 m. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.

Associated colour description: Shell white with a thin, pale horn-brown periostracum, often eroded on spire. Associated size description: Length up to 100 mm. Associated distribution: South African endemic. Agulhas Bank (west of Cape Town to Tsitsikamma), 100–200 m. Extracted image dimensions: 191x191.
